<!-- PostSup Tools — automated technical audit. READ-ONLY: no code was changed. Generated by a multi-agent audit (8 domain specialists + adversarial verification). Review and tell Claude which items to action. -->

# PostSup Tools — Morning Technical Audit

**Date:** 2026-06-04 · **Prepared by:** Lead Technical Director

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The core app is structurally sound and the engineering is genuinely thoughtful (RFC-correct ICS export, careful ACES IDT provenance, sensible graph models). But the **reference-data layer is leaking errors that a working DP, DIT, or focus puller would catch on day one** — fabricated/impossible camera modes, storage rates off by 20–270%, and a handful of glossary facts that are simply wrong. None of these are crashes; all of them quietly produce numbers a professional would trust and then get burned by on set or in the suite.

**46 confirmed findings:** 19 factual errors, 10 logic bugs, 12 UX gaps, 5 enhancements. The highest-leverage cluster is the **storage/codec data-rate library** (media planning runs off it) and the **camera format table** (every framing/DoF/coverage calc reads from it).

### Top 5 to fix first

1. **REDCODE rates ~35% too low + ProRes table shifted one fps notch (~20% high).** Two separate bugs in the same file mean *almost every media-plan estimate is wrong* — RED under-buys cards, ProRes over-buys. Highest blast radius, hits the most jobs. *(formats.ts:1307–1332 and :1202–1235)*
2. **ARRIRAW HDE storage understated ~2.7×.** Modeled as 3.5–4× compression; it is lossless ~1.67:1. A DIT counting mags off this runs out of media mid-shoot. *(formats.ts:1247–1257)*
3. **ALEXA 65 anamorphic mode is physically impossible** (3592 rows on a 3100-row sensor). Corrupts lens-coverage, FOV, and DoF for every downstream consumer. *(formats.ts:239–252)*
4. **6K S35 1.3x anamorphic mode missing its sensor crop → FOV off by ~12°.** The only squeeze>1 entry lacking `usedSensor*`; silently scales the full sensor. *(formats.ts:345–356)*
5. **Schedule "End" date is one day past the real finish on every phase.** PMs book the colourist/suite a day too long on every single deliverable. *(scheduleExport.ts:63, :189)*

---

## 2. Findings by Severity

### Factual Errors (19)

**1. ALEXA 65 "4.3K 4:3 2x Anamorphic" is a physically impossible mode**
*Capture & Framing — `Frame_Matrix/src/lib/formats.ts:239–252` (id `alexa65-2x-ana`)*
Claims 3592 recorded rows and 29.62 mm used-sensor height; the A3X sensor maxes at 3100 rows / 25.58 mm. A sweep of all 22 anamorphic modes flags this as the **only** entry where used-sensor height exceeds the physical sensor. Corrupts `usedSensorDiagonalMm()` → lens-coverage verdict, `computeFovDof` FOV/DoF, the camera report, and file-size estimates.
**Fix:** Replace with a real readout staying ≤3100 rows / ≤25.58 mm (e.g. ~4096×3100 using ~35.1×24.4 mm). Verify against ARRI's ALEXA 65 / Codex format poster.

**2. RED V-RAPTOR S35 "8K S35 4:3 2x Anamorphic" (5760×4320) is a fabricated mode**
*Capture & Framing — `formats.ts:328–344` (id `raptor-s35-8k-43-2x-ana`), incl. comment :329–331*
RED's S35 anamorphic 4:3 tops out at 7K (5040×3780); 5760×4320 exists only on the larger VV sensor. The in-file "Verified vs RED docs" comment is demonstrably false. The lens-coverage check evaluates against a fictional 18.43×13.82 mm area.
**Fix:** Re-label as the real 7K S35 4:3 2x = 5040×3780 (~16.13×12.09 mm), or delete and keep only the VV mode. Fix the misleading comment.

**3. RED V-RAPTOR VV 4:3 anamorphic note self-contradicts ("6:5" vs exact 4:3)**
*Capture & Framing — `formats.ts:284` (notes on `raptor-vv-8k-43-2x-ana`)*
5760/4320 = 1.3333 = exactly 4:3, not 6:5. The "actually 6:5" claim was copy-pasted from the ALEXA 35 (L145) and URSA (L785) entries, where the math genuinely holds (≈1.19). A DP reading the spec sees a contradiction that undermines trust.
**Fix:** Correct this note only to reflect it genuinely is 4:3 (crops 28.80 mm of 40.96 mm width). Leave L145/L785 as-is.

**4. ALEXA LF "1.65x Ana (Hawk65)" attributes the squeeze to the wrong lens system**
*Capture & Framing — `formats.ts:187–200` (id `alexa-lf-165-ana`)*
1.65x is Panavision Ultra Vista; Hawk65 is 1.3x. The app's own LENSES array encodes "Ultra Vista 1.65x" (L1946) and "Hawk65 2x" (L1950) — so the dataset contradicts itself. Pairing 1.65x onto a 16:9 sub-frame also desqueezes to a non-standard 2.93:1.
**Fix:** Rename to "LF 1.65x Ana (Ultra Vista)" and use open-gate geometry (≈4448×3096 → 2.39:1). If a Hawk65 entry is wanted, it should be 1.3x.

**5. ARRIRAW HDE storage understated ~2.7×**
*Storage & Data — `formats.ts:1247–1257` (id `arriraw-hde`)*
Modeled as ~3.5–4× compression (bppx 3.0, ~1.05 Gbps / ~470 GB/hr). ARRI/Codex state HDE is **lossless, ~60% of original (~1.67:1)**. A DIT plans ~2.5–2.7× too few mags and runs out of media on set.
**Fix:** Set HDE to ~60% of uncompressed (bppx ≈7.6–8.0 depending on the uncompressed anchor). Relabel "~2.7–2.8 Gbps @ 4.6K 24p · ~1.2 TB/hr; bit-exact lossless, ~40% smaller (~1.7:1)".

**6. REDCODE HQ/MQ/LQ rates ~30–38% too low; calibration anchor is wrong**
*Storage & Data — `formats.ts:1307–1332`*
Code yields HQ 2206 / MQ 1570 / LQ 1103 Mbps @ 8K 17:9 24p. RED's published figures are HQ 446 / MQ 312 / LQ 195 MB/s (HQ ≈3.57 Gbps). Every REDCODE path (V-RAPTOR, Monstro, Komodo, DXL) under-reports cards and offload time.
**Fix:** Recalibrate to RED spec (HQ bppx ≈4.2, MQ ≈2.95, LQ ≈1.85 for the V1.3 set). Fix the "HQ ≈ 2.25 Gbps" comment to "≈3.57 Gbps @ 8K 17:9 24p".

**7. Entire ProRes rate table is shifted one frame-rate notch (~20% high at 24/25)**
*Storage & Data — `formats.ts:1202–1235` + rateLabels :1264–1304*
Apple's headline rates (422HQ 220, 4444 330 Mbps @1080) are quoted at **29.97 fps** but are placed in the `24` column, with `30` scaled up. True 24/25p figures are ~20% lower (422HQ 1080p25 = 184). At the dominant acquisition rates every ProRes size reads ~20% high.
**Fix:** Shift each row so the headline rate sits in the 29.97/30 column and the ~20%-lower value occupies 24/25. Re-derive UHD/DCI rows from corrected anchors.

**8. ARRIRAW uncompressed ~6% low and bit depth mislabeled (13-bit, not 12-bit)**
*Storage & Data — `formats.ts:1158–1161` (comment) and :1239–1246 (id `arriraw-og`)*
ALEXA 35 records 13-bit log ARRIRAW; code labels 12-bit, bppx 12.0. ARRI's 4644 Mbit/s for 4.6K 3:2 is a **25 fps** figure → implied bppx ≈12.74 (≈5.8% understatement at matched fps, not the 10% one might assume from treating 4644 as 24 fps).
**Fix:** Set bppx ≈12.74, relabel as 13-bit ("~4.46 Gbps @ 24p, 4.64 Gbps @25p · ~2.0 TB/hr"). Note older ALEXAs ≈ bppx 12.

**9. Circle of confusion uses photographic "diagonal/1500" not cinema "width/1500"**
*Optics — `formats.ts:1983` (`cocMm = Math.hypot(sw, sh)/1500`), UI label `FovCalculator.tsx:104`*
Cinema convention (ASC Manual, pCam, Cine Designer) is sensor **width**/1500. Diagonal/1500 is ~21% larger; ALEXA 35 hyperfocal at 35mm f/2.8 reads 19.36 m vs the correct 23.48 m. A focus puller cross-checking pCam sees a consistent mismatch — and the label literally says "sensor Ø ÷ 1500".
**Fix:** Switch to `cocMm = sw / 1500` (used-sensor width, already anamorphic-aware) and fix the UI hint to "sensor width ÷ 1500".

**10. Super-35 equivalence annotated "3-perf" but uses 4-perf dimensions**
*Optics — `FovCalculator.tsx:9` (`S35_DIAG`, comment "Super 35 (3-perf)")*
24.89×18.66 mm (diag 31.1 mm) is 4-perf / full-aperture S35. 3-perf is ~24.89×13.86 mm (diag ~28.5 mm). The number is a valid "full S35" reference; only the comment label is wrong and will mislead anyone reasoning about which S35 the crop factor is relative to.
**Fix:** Change comment to "(4-perf / full-aperture S35)", or switch dims to 24.89×13.86 if 3-perf is intended.

**11. Dolby Vision trim label conflates the SDR derive with an unrelated 600-nit target**
*Mastering & Delivery — `mastering.ts:169` (and dup :344)*
"TID1 trim → SDR Rec.709 100 nit (first), +600 nit". TID1/SDR-100 as the required-first trim is correct, but **600-nit is a separate optional target** (typically a P3 OLED on high-nit masters), not a step in the SDR derive. Appending it to this edge misrepresents the trim model.
**Fix:** Drop the dangling "+600 nit" or move it to its own optional edge listing extra targets (P3 48-nit, PQ 108-nit, ~600-nit P3 OLED).

**12. Dolby Vision Profile 5 is 10-bit, not 12-bit**
*Glossary — `glossary-data.json:2547`*
"Profile 5 is single-layer 12-bit" — it is single-layer **10-bit** HEVC in IPT-PQ-c2. The 12-bit figure describes the master; the whole point of Profile 5 is carrying 12-bit-derived imagery in a 10-bit consumer-decodable stream.
**Fix:** "Profile 5 is single-layer 10-bit in Dolby's IPT-PQ-c2 (Apple TV+, legacy UHD Blu-ray) — not HDR10-compatible." Optionally note the master is 12-bit.

**13. Dolby Vision Profile 8 mislabelled "dual-layer-compatible" when it is single-layer**
*Glossary — `glossary-data.json:2547`*
Profile 8.1/8.4 are **single-layer** (base layer + RPU metadata sidecar). The dual-layer profile is 7 (base + FEL/MEL). Calling 8.x "dual-layer" inverts the key fact.
**Fix:** "both single-layer streaming profiles — backward-compatible base (HDR10/HLG) + DV RPU; contrast with dual-layer Profile 7 (UHD Blu-ray)."

**14. EBU R128 tolerance attributed to the wrong programme type (live/non-live swapped)**
*Glossary — `glossary-data.json:2815`*
"tolerance about ±0.5 LU for live" — backwards. Per DPP standards, **±0.5 LU is non-live/file-based; ±1.0 LU is live** (live can't be re-normalised in post). A dub-stage mixer or QC op would dispute this.
**Fix:** "tolerance ±0.5 LU for non-live/file-based, ±1.0 LU for live; max true peak −1 dBTP."

**15. EBU colour bars: 75% nomenclature but 100% (8-bar) layout — internally inconsistent**
*Glossary — `glossary-data.json:2786`*
"100/0/75/0 ... (eight full-height bars)" contradicts itself. 100/0/75/0 = seven 75% bars; the eight full-field equal-width pattern is 100/0/100/0 (BT.1729). 75% vs 100% bars are materially different references.
**Fix:** Either "100/0/100/0 ... (eight full-height bars)" or "100/0/75/0 ... (seven colour bars plus a 100% white bar)".

**16. "Audio pull" % is inverted — contradicts the +pitch it shows and the pull-up convention**
*Conform math — `postcalc.ts:88` (`audioPullPct = src/tgt * 100`), shown `Tools.tsx:166`*
24→25 PAL plays faster → audio is sped **up** (a "4% pull-up", 25/24 = 104.167%). The tool computes 24/25 = 96.000% and labels it "Audio pull" — reads as a slow-down. It contradicts the adjacent "+0.706 st" pitch stat and the panel's own "pulled up" footnote. Conflict reproduced across all four NTSC/PAL cases.
**Fix:** Compute `audioPullPct = tgt/src * 100` (→104.167% pull-up, matching +0.71 st), or relabel the 96% as "resample to / length vs original". Pull % and semitone sign must agree.

**17. Docs claim "audio-only tracks are skipped" but the EDL path keeps every audio event**
*Conform/converter — `edl.ts:26–55` (parseEDL), comment `sequenceConvert.ts:7–8`, caption `Tools.tsx:334`*
parseEDL retains V, A, A2, AA alike. The audio-skip only happens incidentally on the XML paths (which query `<video>`). The EDL — the format the tool is named after — does no track filtering, so a picture-cut-list user gets phantom audio rows.
**Fix:** Either filter EDL events whose track matches `/^A/i` (opt-in to keep), or remove/soften the "audio-only tracks skipped" claim from the comment and caption.

**18. 1.33 mislabeled "Academy" — Academy aperture is 1.375**
*Aspect ratios — `postcalc.ts:97`*
1.33 is 4:3 (SDTV / silent full aperture); Academy is 1.375 (post-1932 sound aperture). 1.375 isn't in the table at all. A film-literate user — the target audience — will notice.
**Fix:** Rename to "4:3 (1.33 / Full Aperture)" and optionally add `{ r: 1.375, name: "Academy (1.375)" }`. Reserve "Academy" for 1.375.

---

### Logic Bugs (10)

**1. 6K S35 1.3x anamorphic mode missing its used-sensor crop → FOV/DoF badly wrong**
*Optics — `formats.ts:345–356` (id `raptor-s35-6k-179-13x-ana`), consumed `FovCalculator.tsx:18–19`*
The **only** squeeze>1 entry omitting `usedSensorWidthMm/HeightMm` (the other 21 define them). FovCalculator falls back to the full 26.21×13.82 mm sensor then still multiplies by 1.3x. At 35mm: horizontal AOV reads 51.9° instead of 40.1°, vertical 22.3° instead of 16.8°. DoF/CoC wrong by the same proportion.
**Fix:** Add `usedSensorWidthMm: 19.66`, `usedSensorHeightMm: 10.37` (26.21·6144/8192, 13.82·3240/4320). Harden: when squeeze>1 but used-sensor dims are absent, treat as a data error rather than silently scaling the full sensor.

**2. Parallel edges between the same two nodes draw and label on top of each other**
*Mastering — `MasteringWorkflow.tsx:288–304` (paths) & :308–331 (chips); data `mastering.ts:166–180`*
Edge geometry derives only from endpoint positions, no offset for parallel edges. `hdrHero→dvxml` has both an "analyze" (L1) and a "trim" edge; both Béziers and both chips render at identical coordinates (same grey-dotted style), so one operation is hidden and the chips stack unreadably — worst when a node is selected and both labels expand.
**Fix:** Group edges by (from,to), compute an in-group index, apply a perpendicular offset (±14px) to control points and chip midpoint. Alternatively merge analyze+trim into one labelled edge.

**3. VFX finals loop backward into the pre-lock Network/Studio Cut**
*Workflow — `pipeline.ts:230` (`v-master → e-netcut`)*
The VFX subgraph is triggered by Picture Lock, yet the approved master (order 6) routes back to e-netcut (order 5), which **precedes** the lock that triggered it — the only non-back-op edge running backward in stage order, closing a real cycle. Correct destinations already exist (`v-master → c-timeline`). v-master's own detail says "Overrides the original plate at conform," never "back into the cut."
**Fix:** Remove the `v-master → e-netcut` edge. If temp VFX updating the offline is intended, model it from v-comp (WIP) into editorial, not from the approved master.

**4. lookupProResRate assumes 16:9, so DCI 4K/8K rows undershoot their own rate ~6%**
*Storage — `formats.ts:1780` inside `lookupProResRate`*
`refPx = bestW * (bestW * 9/16)` forces 16:9. DCI-4K is 4096×2160 (~1.90:1) and 8K-DCI is 8192×4320, so a native-DCI request gives askPx/refPx = 0.9375 → 707 Mbps instead of 754 (422HQ DCI 4K), 2827 vs 3015 at 8K. Stacks on the fps-axis error.
**Fix:** Store each row's actual reference height (4096→2160, 8192→4320; HD/UHD keep 9/16) so native requests return the published rate exactly.

**5. CSV/PDF/on-screen "End" date is the exclusive end — one day past the real finish**
*Scheduler — `scheduleExport.ts:63` (buildCSV), :189 (bar label); `PostSchedule.tsx:526,571`*
"End" = `dateAtISO(anchor, start+dur)` = the Monday the **next** phase begins. A 3-week grade from Mon 01 Jun shows "End: 22 Jun" but the last working day is Sun 21 Jun. The ICS export correctly uses this exclusive value (RFC 5545 §3.8.2.2), but every PM tool reads "End" as the last inclusive day, so the suite/colourist is booked a day too long on every phase.
**Fix:** Keep ICS DTEND as-is. For CSV/PDF/tooltip subtract one day (`addDaysISO(..., -1)`) so "End" is the inclusive last day. Alternatively rename to "Ends before".

**6. Duration editor caps at 104 weeks but canvas/export hard-caps at 80**
*Scheduler — `PostSchedule.tsx:140` (`weeksToShow = min(…, 80)`), :613–616 (input max 104); `scheduleExport.ts:103,124,136`*
A 104-week phase renders only its first 80 weeks; the tail runs off-edge. The PNG/PDF canvas width derives from the same capped value, so the **exported** Gantt silently drops the back end of any long track (late 13-week VFX, a 2-year feature). Resize-right (:181) has no upper clamp at all.
**Fix:** Raise/remove the 80 cap so `renderGanttCanvas` spans every bar (e.g. `min(max(ceil(maxEnd)+6, 24), 120)`). Add an upper clamp to the resize handler to mirror the 104 editor limit.

**7. FCPXML source in/out double-counts the asset's start time (often ~1h)**
*Converter — `sequenceConvert.ts:124` (`srcBase = (asset.start||0) + start`), used :130–131*
The asset-clip's `start` is already in the asset's coordinate space (same origin as `asset.start`). Adding both double-counts the origin. For media with embedded source TC (asset start = 3600s = 01:00:00:00, typical for camera/broadcast), every Src In/Out is offset high by a full hour+. Files with asset.start=0 happen to come out right, masking the bug.
**Fix:** Use `srcBase = start` directly; fall back to asset.start only when no start attribute exists. Keep the "experimental" label, but default math should match the spec.

**8. Drop-frame parser silently accepts non-existent DF timecodes**
*Converter — `postcalc.ts:31–42` (tcToFrames) / :22–28 (parseTC)*
In 29.97/59.94 DF, :00/:01 at non-tenth minutes don't exist. tcToFrames maps `00:01:00;00` → 1798, but framesToTC(1798) = `00:00:59;28` — non-injective, round-trips to a different label than typed. A pasted illegal value shifts the frame count silently (up to 2 frames per minute boundary in an EDL conform).
**Fix:** When df is true, reject `f < dropFrames` where `m%10 !== 0 && s === 0` (return null) so illegal labels surface as invalid rather than mapping to an adjacent frame.

**9. EDL writer emits malformed single-line dissolves although it only produces cuts**
*Converter — `sequenceConvert.ts:189–199` (toEDL)*
(a) `const trans = (e.transition||'C') + (e.transDur ? '' : '')` is a no-op — both branches append `''`. (b) A `D 025` event is written as a lone dissolve line; a valid CMX3600 dissolve needs **two** synchronised lines (cut to outgoing, then `D nnn` to incoming). The output is neither a clean cut list (contract says "transitions become cuts") nor a valid dissolve.
**Fix:** Pick one behaviour: force `C` and drop transDur on EDL output, or emit the proper two-line dissolve. Delete the no-op ternary.

**10. xmeml in/out treated as inclusive but FCP7/Premiere out-points are exclusive**
*Converter — `sequenceConvert.ts:79–82`*
xmeml `<out>`/`<end>` are exclusive (one past last frame; out/end use 1-based numbering vs 0-based in/start). Mapping `<end>` straight into an EDL out-point yields a duration one frame long per event — exactly the discrepancy that fails a conform QC when reconciling against the original timeline.
**Fix:** Subtract 1 frame from xmeml-derived out/end for EDL durations (or clearly state outputs use exclusive out-points). Apply consistently to FCPXML (`srcOut = srcBase + duration` is also one-past-last).

---

### UX Gaps (12)

**1. Two camera modes share the identical name "VENICE 2 · 5.8K 6:5 2x Anamorphic"**
*Capture & Framing — `formats.ts:437–450` (5792×4854) AND :470–487 (5760×4800)*
Byte-identical mode labels, different dimensions and used-sensor areas — indistinguishable in the dropdown, and they produce different framing charts/FDLs/file sizes. Sony's published 5.8K 6:5 is 5792×4854 (matching the first entry and BURANO); the 5760×4800 duplicate is **not** a Sony resolution, so it also carries an embedded factual error.
**Fix:** Remove the duplicate `venice2-58k-65-2x` (5760×4800); keep `venice2-43-2x` (5792×4854) as canonical. Optionally add Sony's real 5.8K 17:9 (5792×3056).

**2. DoF treats the T-stop input as a geometric f-number with no caveat**
*Optics — `FovCalculator.tsx:64–65` (slider "Aperture (T/f)"), :27; math `formats.ts:1984–1987`*
DoF is governed by the physical f-number, not the T-stop; on fast cine glass a T2.8 lens is ~f/2.5. The combined "T/f" label feeds the marked T-stop straight into the math, giving optimistically deep DoF for a pro reading the barrel.
**Fix:** Either label "f-number" with a note (enter the lens's f-number, ~⅓-stop faster than its T-stop), or keep "T-stop" and disclose that DoF treats T as f. A one-line hint under the DoF panel closes it.

**3. Media-plan "fill %" exceeds 100% on multi-card days**
*Storage — `FileSizeCalculator.tsx:397` (`utilisation = perCamGB/cardGB*100`), :411*
`cardsPerCamPerDay` (:392) already ceils to multiple cards, but utilisation ignores that. 1.5 TB/day on a 1 TB card reads "150% fill" — nonsense to the AC/DIT. True average is 75%.
**Fix:** `utilisation = perCamGB / (cardsPerCamPerDay × cardGB) × 100`, optionally annotating the last card's partial fill. Caps at ≤100%.

**4. Delivery DAG omits loudness/LKFS targets entirely**
*Mastering — `mastering.ts` (whole file) & `MasteringWorkflow.tsx`; specs exist in `pipeline.ts:149`*
The picture-deliverables map carries zero audio-loudness spec, though the correct strings already exist elsewhere (Netflix −27 LKFS / −2 dBTP; EBU R128 −23 LUFS / −1 dBTP; ATSC A/85 −24 LUFS / −2 dBTP). The view reads as a complete deliverable spec but is picture-only. *(Defensible that picture/audio are split into separate stages, but the IMF/broadcast nodes carry audio in reality.)*
**Fix:** Add the loudness/true-peak target to each deliverable node, reusing the `pipeline.ts:149` strings so the two views stay consistent.

**5. SDR IMF labelled "App 2" on deprecated ST 2067-20**
*Mastering — `mastering.ts:155` (imfsdr node)*
SMPTE's 2020 revision folded App 2 (ST 2067-20) into App 2E (ST 2067-21); App 2 is deprecated. Real Netflix/studio delivery carries SDR+HDR within App 2E, so presenting App 2 as a current separate deliverable can mislead planning. (The ST number is historically correct — a currency/clarity issue.)
**Fix:** Relabel "IMF App 2E — SDR Rec.709 (ST 2067-21)", or annotate App 2 as a legacy path.

**6. No HLG deliverable path despite HLG being first-class elsewhere in the app**
*Mastering — `mastering.ts` (no HLG node); cf. `Index.tsx:199` HDR_VARIANTS, `aces.ts:160–167` HLG_ODT*
The HDR variant selector lists HLG and aces.ts has a full HLG ODT, but the Mastering DAG can't model an HLG master in any strategy or the custom builder — a coverage gap vs the app's own capabilities. *(Somewhat defensible since this DAG targets streaming/theatrical and HLG is broadcast-centric.)*
**Fix:** Add an optional HLG deliverable (lateral PQ→HLG conversion edge or a custom-deliverable toggle), reusing the aces.ts HLG ODT label.

**7. HDR10 / IMF-App-2E peak scales to mastering-display nits without showing the MaxCLL/MaxFALL consequence**
*Mastering — `mastering.ts:248` (NITS_NODES) & :263, :301/:341*
Selecting a 2000/4000-nit display re-labels the HDR10 and App 2E nodes to that peak (defensible — tracks ST 2086 metadata), but nothing tells the planner that MaxCLL/MaxFALL change and many specs still expect a 1000-nit MaxCLL target. A pro could read "4000 nit" as a delivery requirement rather than a mastering-display artefact. *(Behaviour is internally consistent — a clarity gap.)*
**Fix:** Add a note on hdr10/imf2e that the peak follows the mastering display, MaxCLL/MaxFALL are content-measured, and to confirm the delivery's MaxCLL target (often 1000 nit).

**8. Audio Conform claims a "FINAL graded picture" dependency that no edge feeds — and the premise is wrong**
*Workflow — `pipeline.ts:150` (a-conform), :243 (a-loud → a-conform)*
a-conform's prose asserts a dependency on the graded master, but its only inbound is a-loud (audio) and the sole picture→audio edge is e-turnover → a-spot. Crucially, audio conform cares about **length/fps/sync** (fixed at online), not the grade — mixes routinely finish parallel to grading. So the absence of a grade→audio edge is correct; the defect is the word "graded."
**Fix:** Soften wording to "final conformed/locked picture (frame-exact length, project fps)". Optionally add a non-blocking dashed reference edge from c-timeline. Do **not** add a hard grade→audio dependency or reorder bands.

**9. "Synced Dailies" is a dead-end; the cut is built from a picture-only proxy with no sound**
*Workflow — `pipeline.ts:116` (d-synced), :202; vs :115 (d-proxy), :204*
d-synced ("picture married to sound") has out-degree 0; e-assembly is fed solely by the silent d-proxy. But offlines are always cut **with** synced sound (the guide track). (a-sync is a near-duplicate in meaning but is **not** a dead-end — it correctly feeds a-dx at :215.)
**Fix:** Wire d-synced → e-assembly, or fold d-synced into d-proxy so the feeding proxy is the synced one. Reconcile the wording mismatch between line 77 and the :202 label.

**10. EDL/FCM drop-frame flag is one value for the whole sequence; per-event FCM switches are lost**
*Converter — `edl.ts:36 & 54` (only last FCM kept), `sequenceConvert.ts:185`*
CMX3600 allows FCM: lines to switch DF mid-list (mixed-rate/DF reels). parseEDL keeps only the last FCM; parseSequence applies one df to the whole sequence, so a mixed EDL is re-timecoded under a single DF assumption and gets one FCM header on export.
**Fix:** Track FCM per event and honour it in round-trips; emit an FCM line only on change. At minimum, warn when more than one FCM appears.

**11. EDL writer emits reel names >8 chars by default, breaking strict CMX3600 ingest**
*Converter — `sequenceConvert.ts:192–195` (toEDL, `opts.reelLong`), default ON at `Tools.tsx:249`*
True CMX3600 reels are 8 chars in a fixed column. The "reel > 8 chars" option defaults ON, so a 16-char reel writes unpadded and shifts every subsequent column; column-position parsers reject or misread the line.
**Fix:** Default `reelLong` to false (8-char truncation); keep the long form as an explicit opt-in for Resolve/extended EDL. Optionally warn that long reels are non-standard.

**12. Pasted CSV without reel/clip/src in the header is misdetected as EDL → "0 events", no reason given**
*Converter — `sequenceConvert.ts:28–38` (detectFormat), :36*
A valid comma-separated cut list with differently-named headers (or a leading index column) falls through to "edl", parseEDL finds nothing, and the user sees "No events found" mislabeled as CMX3600 — the most common real-world failure mode, with no hint it was parsed as the wrong format.
**Fix:** Broaden the CSV fallback (no XML/EDL signature + commas + consistent column count across rows → CSV). Surface the detected format and, on 0 events, suggest trying a different format rather than failing silently.

---

### Enhancements (5)

**1. JSON "backup" export has no matching import — a backup you can never restore**
*Scheduler — `PostSchedule.tsx:59` (label "JSON — backup"); `scheduleExport.ts:54–56,201`*
"Backup" implies save-and-reload, but there is no file-import path — loadBars only reads localStorage, and Versions are localStorage-only. Clear the browser or switch machines and the downloaded JSON is dead weight: a data-loss trap dressed as a safety net for a freelancer moving between prep laptop and edit suite.
**Fix:** Add a "JSON — backup" UX gap is severe enough to consider higher priority — add an Import JSON (`input[type=file]` → `setBars`/`setStartDate`, reusing loadBars validation), or relabel to "JSON — data export". Note buildJSON also omits `anchor`, so any importer must re-derive the timeline from startDate.

**2. Version load does no per-bar validation, unlike loadBars — a corrupt version can break drag/keys**
*Scheduler — `PostSchedule.tsx:47–52` (loadVersions), :342–345 (loadVersion); cf. :105–115 (loadBars)*
loadBars filters each bar; loadVersion does `setBars(v.bars.map(b => ({...b})))` with no filtering. A bar missing `id` becomes key=undefined and throws in drag handlers (`onDown :211` uses a non-null assertion). loadBars itself also doesn't require id/color. *(Trigger needs external corruption — the app's own saveVersion always writes valid bars.)*
**Fix:** Run version bars through the loadBars sanitiser and backfill `id` (uid()) and `color` (PALETTE) in both loaders.

**3. Template/new-phase "current week" uses Math.round, landing a week late on Fri–Sun**
*Scheduler — `PostSchedule.tsx:319` (newStart), :330–331 (applyTemplate)*
UI promises "starting from the current week", but `Math.round(todayWeeks)` rounds **Fri/Sat/Sun** (0.571/0.714/0.857) up to the next week. *(Mon–Thu are fine — Thursday=0.429 rounds down, contrary to a naive reading.)* Adding a phase on a weekend silently starts it next Monday.
**Fix:** Use `Math.floor(todayWeeks)` at :319 and :330 so "current week" is always the Monday of today's week.

**4. Anamorphic CoC computed on squeezed photosites, not the desqueezed delivery frame**
*Optics — `formats.ts:1983` with anamorphic used-sensor dims*
For anamorphic, sw/sh are the squeezed dimensions; the audience sees the ~2× wider desqueezed frame, so a blur circle is magnified more horizontally than the squeezed CoC implies → DoF errs slightly optimistic on high-squeeze shows. Refinement, not a correctness break (most pocket calculators ignore it).
**Fix:** Optionally compute CoC from the desqueezed width `(sw*sq)/1500` (coheres fully once the width-based CoC of Finding 9 is adopted), or note in the UI that DoF is on the captured frame and is a touch optimistic for high-squeeze anamorphic.

**5. Fractional/garbage frame entries are silently rounded/accepted**
*Converter — `Tools.tsx:95` (`frInt = Math.round(Number(frames))`), :88–89; `postcalc.ts:47`*
The Frames input accepts "12.9" (→13), "1e6", and empty (→0 → shows 00:00:00:00). Frame counts are integers; silently rounding or treating blank as 0 masks a typo for a careful conform. Low impact but unprofessional for a precision tool.
**Fix:** Reject non-integer/empty input (accept only `/^\d+$/`) and treat empty as no-result rather than 0.

*(Also noted, no action required: the **Apple Log IDT** is correctly flagged official — verified against the live AMPAS repo, which now ships both Apple Log and Apple Log 2 IDTs. The `aces.ts` header still says "mid-2025"; Apple Log 2 post-dates that, so optionally bump the provenance line. The **HLG ODT note** ("ONLY a P3-D65-limited HLG") and the **ACEScct / ACES2065-1** hints are all factually load-bearing-correct — minor wording polish only, captured implicitly above where relevant.)*

---

## 3. What's Already Solid

- **The hard math is right where it counts.** Timecode round-trips for legal DF values, the ICS export's RFC 5545 DTEND handling, and the FOV/DoF formulas themselves are correct — the failures are all *data* (wrong constants, missing crops), not *algorithms*.
- **ACES & colour is in genuinely good shape.** Apple Log / Apple Log 2 IDT provenance, the N-Log/F-Log2 `official:false` flags, and the HLG/ACEScct/ACES2065-1 facts were all verified accurate against the live AMPAS repos. Only editorial polish remains.
- **The workflow and mastering graphs are professionally modelled.** Dead-end "review-only" leaf nodes, the picture/audio stage split, and the lock-triggered VFX pull all reflect real post practice — the few defects are single mis-wired edges and overstated node prose, not a flawed mental model.
- **Defensive patterns already exist** (loadBars sanitises bars; the format table mostly defines `usedSensor*` correctly; §B.3/§B.4 comments show an earlier pass already fixed a class of impossible camera modes) — the remaining issues are the stragglers that slipped that net, which makes them cheap to close by following the patterns already in the codebase.

**Bottom line:** No fires, but the camera-format and codec-rate tables need a focused correction pass before any of these numbers are put in front of a client. Fix the Top 5 and the tool moves from "looks authoritative" to "is authoritative."
